title: Deliverable Card v1
description: One JSON per project deliverable — a tangible or measurable output the project must produce and that someone must accept. Unlike a milestone (a moment), a deliverable is a thing, with a format, an owner, a recipient, acceptance criteria, and a sign-off. Deliverables roll up to a milestone and are produced by action-cards. Lives in <project>/deliverables/.

format.description: What form it takes, e.g. 'document', 'signed-off runbook', 'deployed service', 'training session'.

owner.description: Who is accountable for producing it.

recipient.description: Who receives and accepts it (the party whose sign-off counts).

milestone_id.description: The milestone this deliverable rolls up to, if any.

baseline_date.description: Originally baselined due date, for variance tracking.

status.description: in-review=submitted to the recipient, awaiting acceptance; accepted=signed off; rejected=sent back, needs rework.

acceptance_criteria.description: The pre-agreed standard the deliverable is judged against. A deliverable with no acceptance criteria cannot be objectively accepted — this is required for exactly that reason.

contact_id.description: Optional contact-card reference for the accepting person.

sign_off.description: Set when status=accepted: the record of who accepted it and when.

rejection_note.description: Why a deliverable was sent back. Set when status=rejected so the reason isn't lost.

issues.description: Issue-card ids raised against this deliverable — the deliverable → issue → action chain (the issues' own action_cards[] carry the work).

updated.description: Date this card was last touched.
